# Tril

> Convert any codebase into plain English that an LLM can execute.

Tril is a CLI tool that transforms code repositories into natural language programs. It replaces every function with structured English descriptions — Purpose, Inputs, Behaviour, Output, Edge cases, Examples — that an LLM can execute via `claude -p`.

## Commands

- `tril convert <source>` — Convert a local directory or GitHub URL into `.md` files
- `tril run <dir>` — Run a converted natural language program via LLM

## How It Works

1. **Convert**: Scans source code (JS/Python), extracts functions, translates each to structured English via Claude
2. **Run**: Reads `.md` files, starts HTTP server, routes business logic through `claude -p`

## Key Concept

Code is compressed human language. Tril decompresses it. The converted repo is plain markdown — readable by anyone, renderable on GitHub, and executable by an LLM.

## Links

- Website: https://tril.cc
- Repository: https://github.com/sliday/tril
- Examples: https://github.com/sliday/tril/tree/main/examples

## Requirements

- Claude Code CLI installed and authenticated
- Node.js 18+
